pi_items.source: setup_task.sh audit report section listing FOREST 1 and FOREST 2 domains
setup_task.sh audit report section 'CONFIGURED TRUST RELATIONSHIPS' item 2 specifies bidirectional forest trust between contoso.com and fabrikam.net
setup_task.sh audit report section 'CONFIGURED TRUST RELATIONSHIPS' item 3 identifies 'Trusting Domain: corp.contoso.com'
setup_task.sh audit report section 'CONFIGURED TRUST RELATIONSHIPS' item 3 identifies 'Trusted Domain: legacy.fabrikam.net'
setup_task.sh specifies 'Direction: Two-way / Bidirectional' and Microsoft AD documentation confirms forest trusts are transitive
setup_task.sh specifies 'Direction: One-way (Incoming to legacy)' and audit report notes 'Non-transitive'; Microsoft AD documentation confirms external trusts are one-way and non-transitive
setup_task.sh audit report 'Forest Functional Level: Windows Server 2016' for FOREST 1: CONTOSO.COM
setup_task.sh audit report 'Forest Functional Level: Windows Server 2012 R2' for FOREST 2: FABRIKAM.NET
install_drawio.sh line 67 specifies DRAWIO_VERSION="26.0.9"; verified via GitHub releases showing v26.0.9 released January 27, 2025

privileged_info_summary: The task involves creating an Active Directory topology diagram with synthetic but realistic data. There are exactly 5 domains: contoso.com (Contoso forest root), corp.contoso.com (child), research.contoso.com (child), fabrikam.net (Fabrikam forest root), and legacy.fabrikam.net (child). The diagram must show two bidirectional forest trusts (one within each forest for parent-child relationships, and one between forest roots contoso.com and fabrikam.net). An external one-way trust from corp.contoso.com (trusting domain) to legacy.fabrikam.net (trusted domain) must be included with the arrow pointing to the trusted domain. The task uses draw.io Desktop version 26.0.9. Output files must be saved as ad_topology.drawio and ad_topology.png on the Desktop. The audit report specifies Contoso forest at Windows Server 2016 functional level and Fabrikam forest at Windows Server 2012 R2.
